checks.label: Boot < 500ms
checks.description: Server cold-starts and responds to the first health-check request within 500 milliseconds
checks.explanation_markdown: This check verifies that the agent boots and responds to the session/new request within 500ms.
checks.message: Copilot failed Boot < 500ms in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Listing session modes
checks.description: Advertises supported modes (e.g., Ask/Plan/Build) through session config options
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that the agent supports modes through Session Config Options.

Why is this important?

Clients need this information to render the current mode (for example Ask/Plan/Build) in the UI.

Agents that support modes should expose a valid single-select session config option for modes, including the current value and the available values that clients can present to users.
checks.message: Copilot passed Listing session modes in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Switching modes
checks.description: Allows switching modes (e.g. Plan/Ask/Build) through session config options
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that the agent can switch modes using ACP Session Config Options.

Why is this important?

Clients need reliable mode switching to implement shortcuts like Shift+Tab. If an agent exposes a mode config option with multiple values, it should accept another valid value via session/set_config_option and keep the session usable afterward.
checks.message: Copilot passed Switching modes in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Switch mode < 500ms
checks.description: Changes the mode session configuration option within 500 milliseconds
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that the agent responds to a mode config option change within 500ms.

Why is this important?

Mode switching is often exposed through keyboard shortcuts like Shift+Tab, so changing the mode config option should feel instantaneous. Slow config option updates make the client feel laggy and can interrupt the user's flow.
checks.message: Copilot passed Switch mode < 500ms in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Loads skills
checks.description: Loads skills from `.agents/skills` in the working directory as slash commands
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that when a session is created with a working directory containing .agents/skills, the agent advertises those skills through Slash Commands.

Why is this important?

Clients use slash command updates to show users which project-specific skills are available in the current workspace.

Agents should discover skills from the session working directory and send an available_commands_update notification that includes each user-invocable skill.
checks.message: Copilot failed Loads skills in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Loads skills < 500ms
checks.description: Loads `.agents/skills` slash commands within 500 milliseconds of session creation
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that skills from the session working directory are advertised as slash commands within 500ms of calling session/new.

Why is this important?

Project skills affect the commands a client presents immediately after a session starts. Slow skill discovery can make command menus feel incomplete or laggy.
checks.message: Copilot failed Loads skills < 500ms in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Session close
checks.description: Properly handles session termination by cleaning up resources and acknowledging the close request
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that the agent supports the session/close capability.

Why is this important?

session/close is required to terminate a running session and free up resources.
checks.message: Copilot failed Session close in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Session resume
checks.description: Can resume a previously started session and restore its state without losing context
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that the agent supports the session/resume capability.

Why is this important?

session/resume allows clients to reconnect to a previously created session without losing conversation history.
checks.message: Copilot failed Session resume in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Executes terminal commands
checks.description: Uses the ACP terminal capability to run commands in the client environment
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that when the client advertises terminal support, the agent can execute a command through the ACP terminal/create method.

Why is this important?

Agents often need to run tests, builds, formatters, and other command-line tools. Using ACP terminals lets clients keep command execution in the user's environment while retaining visibility and control over the process.
checks.message: Copilot failed Executes terminal commands in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Streams terminal output
checks.description: Polls terminal output while commands are running so command progress can be streamed
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that after creating a terminal, the agent requests the command's output through terminal/output.

Why is this important?

Long-running commands should not feel like black boxes. Polling terminal output lets the agent observe progress, react to failures, and keep users informed while work is still in progress.
checks.message: Copilot failed Streams terminal output in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Displays running terminal command
checks.description: Embeds the active terminal in an in-progress execute tool call
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that the agent includes terminal content in a tool_call or tool_call_update while the command is in progress.

Why is this important?

Clients can only render a live terminal UI when the agent embeds the terminal ID in a tool call. This gives users immediate visibility into which command is running and where to watch its progress.
checks.message: Copilot failed Displays running terminal command in the latest verifier run.

checks.label: Displays terminal output
checks.description: Leaves terminal output visible to the user through terminal content embedded in tool calls
checks.explanation_markdown:
This check verifies that terminal output remains displayable by embedding the terminal in a tool call whose output contains the command result.

Why is this important?

Users need to see the command output that led to the agent's next steps, especially for test failures, build errors, and generated logs. Terminal content in tool calls lets clients display this output directly in the conversation.
checks.message: Copilot failed Displays terminal output in the latest verifier run.
